title: Simple Mail Transfer Protocol
description: The standard protocol for sending email between mail servers and from clients to servers.
fullExplanation: SMTP is a push protocol — it sends mail from client to server or between servers. It uses a simple text-based command/response model. Port 25 is for server-to-server relay. Port 587 (STARTTLS) is for client submission. Port 465 is for SMTPS (implicit TLS). SMTP only sends mail — receiving uses IMAP (port 993) or POP3 (port 995). Modern SMTP requires authentication (SMTP AUTH) and encryption (STARTTLS or TLS).

commands.description: Extended HELO — identify client, get server capabilities
Begin message body (headers + body, end with single dot)

emailAuthentication.description: Sender Policy Framework — DNS TXT record listing authorized sending IPs
DomainKeys Identified Mail — cryptographic signature in email header
Domain-based Message Authentication — policy for SPF/DKIM failures (reject/quarantine)
Authenticated Received Chain — preserves auth results through forwarding

keyFacts: SMTP is push-only — use IMAP/POP3 to receive mail
Port 25 blocked by most ISPs to prevent spam
Port 587 + STARTTLS is the standard for client submission
SPF, DKIM, DMARC prevent email spoofing and phishing
Open relay: SMTP server that forwards mail for anyone — major spam source
SMTP AUTH required for client authentication
Greylisting: temporarily reject unknown senders (spam reduction)
MX records in DNS point to mail servers for a domain

versions.feature: Extended SMTP — EHLO, AUTH, SIZE, STARTTLS
